description.content: Quick check that booking-service is reachable.
Quick check that ride-service is reachable.

Run auth first, then driver profile with vehicleType BIKE/CAR4/CAR7, driver online + GPS. Matching is Kafka async, so after create booking wait 2-5 seconds then poll Booking Active. Redis CLI checks: GET driver:status:{driverUserId}, GET driver:vehicleType:{driverUserId}, GEOPOS driver:locations {driverUserId}.
Driver Service owns driver profile. vehicleType must be one of BIKE, CAR4, CAR7.

body.raw:
{
  "fullName": "Boundary Flow Driver",
  "email": "{{driverEmail}}",
  "phoneNumber": "0902000002",
  "avatarUrl": "https://example.com/driver.png",
  "licenseNumber": "GPLX-BOUNDARY-001",
  "vehicleType": "{{vehicleType}}",
  "vehiclePlate": "51A-99999",
  "vehicleModel": "Toyota Vios",
  "vehicleColor": "White",
  "serviceArea": "Ho Chi Minh City"
}

item.name: 2.2 Set Driver ONLINE -> Redis driver:status AVAILABLE

description.content: Expected side effect: Driver Service writes driver:status:{{driverUserId}} = AVAILABLE. Matching also requires driver vehicle metadata: driver:vehicleType:{{driverUserId}} = {{vehicleType}}.

item.name: 2.3 Push Driver GPS To Ride Redis GEO

description.content: Ride Service owns driver:locations. Expected Redis: GEOPOS driver:locations {{driverUserId}}.
Lay quote moi tu Pricing-Service. Test script luu estimateId, quoteId, quotePayloadHash, quoteHashAlgorithm, expiresAt va totalFare vao collection variables de request tao booking dung ngay sau do.
Expected async: Booking MATCHING -> ride.created(vehicleType) -> Matching reads Redis status/GEO/vehicleType -> driver.assigned canonical + ride.assigned legacy -> Booking ASSIGNED.

item.name: 3.2 Poll Driver Current Ride After driver.assigned

description.content: Run after waiting a few seconds. If Driver Service is not consuming assignment events directly, this can still be empty; Booking Active is the source of truth for ASSIGNED.
Driver Service publishes driver.accepted; Booking consumes and updates ASSIGNED -> ACCEPTED. Driver writes driver:status BUSY.
Expected status ACCEPTED after Kafka processing.
Booking marks the booking CANCELLED. Driver Service no longer owns ride lifecycle cleanup, so the next request releases the driver's local accepted ride for this demo driver.

Demo cleanup for reusing one driver in the collection: clears driver-service currentRide after customer cancellation. Booking and Matching should ignore this late driver.rejected because the booking is already CANCELLED.

item.name: 4.3 Poll Driver Current Ride After Cancel Cleanup

description.content: Expected no current ride / null rideId; Redis driver:status should be AVAILABLE.

item.name: 5.2 Poll Driver Current Ride After Reject Assignment

description.content: Run after a short wait. Expected rideId == {{rejectBookingId}} if this driver was assigned.

Driver Service publishes driver.rejected. Booking consumes it and moves ASSIGNED -> MATCHING. Matching Service consumes driver.rejected, excludes the rejected driver, and rematches from its cached ride.created snapshot.
Expected status MATCHING if no other driver is available, or ASSIGNED if another driver was found.
Lay quote moi tu Pricing-Service. Test script luu estimateId, quoteId, quotePayloadHash, quoteHashAlgorithm, expiresAt va totalFare vao collection variables de request tao booking dung ngay sau do.

description.content: Ride Service validates the JWT driver is assigned to this ride, updates ACCEPTED -> PICKUP, and publishes ride.arrived.
Ride Service validates the JWT driver is assigned to this ride, updates PICKUP -> IN_PROGRESS, and publishes ride.started.
Ride Service validates the JWT driver is assigned to this ride, updates IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED, and publishes ride.completed. Booking consumes ride.completed and updates COMPLETED.

Creates a CASH booking for the full E2E payment flow. Wait 2-5 seconds after this request so Matching can assign the driver asynchronously.

exec: pm.test('7.8 assigned driver is current logged-in driver', () => { const assignedDriverId = pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eCashAssignedDriverId'); if (assignedDriverId) pm.expect(assignedDriverId).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('driverUserId')); });

item.name: 7.9 Poll Driver Current Ride After Assignment

description.content: Expected immediately after Matching assignment: current ride points at the E2E booking for the same driver token used by this collection. If booking.assignedDriverId differs from driverUserId, Matching selected another online driver from Redis; set other drivers offline/clear stale Redis statuses or use that assigned driver token. Assignment TTL is 30 seconds.

exec: pm.test('7.9 current ride poll returns ok/not-found while async', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 404]));

pm.test('7.9 booking was assigned to this logged-in driver', () => { const assignedDriverId = pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eCashAssignedDriverId'); if (assignedDriverId) pm.expect(assignedDriverId).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('driverUserId')); });
pm.test('7.9 driver has assigned ride before timeout', () => pm.expect(rideId, 'rideId from driver current-ride').to.not.be.empty);

description.content: Driver Service accepts the assigned job through the assignment command and publishes ride.accepted. Ride Service consumes ride.accepted and owns the ASSIGNED -> ACCEPTED ride-state transition before arrive/start/complete. Booking also consumes the event. Wait 1-3 seconds before ride lifecycle requests.

exec: pm.test('7.10 booking assigned driver matches current driver', () => { const assignedDriverId = pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eCashAssignedDriverId'); if (assignedDriverId) pm.expect(assignedDriverId).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('driverUserId')); });
pm.test('7.10 booking id exists before accept', () => pm.expect(pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eCashBookingId')).to.not.be.empty);

description.content: Ride Service validates assigned driver, updates ACCEPTED -> PICKUP, and publishes ride.arrived.
Ride Service validates assigned driver, updates PICKUP -> IN_PROGRESS, and publishes ride.started.
Ride Service validates assigned driver, updates IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED, and publishes ride.completed. Wait 2-5 seconds afterward for Booking and Payment consumers.

exec: pm.test('ride completed status returned when present', () => { if (result.status) pm.expect(result.status).to.eql('COMPLETED'); });

description.content: Expected after ride.completed: payment-service records CASH payment and publishes payment.completed.
Expected completed booking appears in customer history with status COMPLETED. CASH payment success is verified separately through Payment Service after ride.completed.

exec: const list = Array.isArray(result) ? result : (result.content || result.data || result.items || []);
const booking = list.find(b => (b.id || b.bookingId || b.rideId) === bookingId);
pm.test('7.15 booking appears in history', () => pm.expect(booking, 'booking in history').to.exist);
pm.test('7.15 booking payment method is CASH', () => pm.expect(booking.paymentMethod).to.eql('CASH'));
pm.test('7.15 booking final status is COMPLETED', () => pm.expect(booking.status).to.eql('COMPLETED'));

description.content: Expected after ride.completed consumer: no active current ride for the driver. If Kafka is still processing, wait and retry.

exec: pm.test('7.16 driver current ride released or still processing', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 404]));
pm.test('7.16 current ride empty when cleanup finished', () => pm.expect(rideId || '').to.be.oneOf(['', null]));

Lay quote moi tu Pricing-Service. Test script luu estimateId, quoteId, quotePayloadHash, quoteHashAlgorithm, expiresAt va totalFare vao collection variables de request tao booking dung ngay sau do.

Creates a VNPAY prepaid booking. Expected async: Booking CREATED -> PENDING_PAYMENT, then publishes payment.requested. Payment Service consumes payment.requested and creates the VNPay transaction. Matching must not start until payment.completed is published.

body.raw:
{
  "pickupLocation": "Landmark 81, Binh Thanh, Ho Chi Minh City",
  "dropoffLocation": "Saigon Opera House",
  "customerNote": "E2E VNPay payment flow",
  "pickupCoordinates": {
    "lat": 10.7769,
    "lng": 106.7009
  },
  "dropoffCoordinates": {
    "lat": 10.7765,
    "lng": 106.7039
  },
  "vehicleType": "{{vehicleType}}",
  "paymentMethod": "VNPAY",
  "estimatedFare": {{estimatedFare}},
  "promoCode": "E2E_VNPAY",
  "quoteToken": "{{quoteToken}}",
  "estimateId": "{{estimateId}}",
  "quoteId": "{{quoteId}}",
  "quotePayloadHash": "{{quotePayloadHash}}",
  "quoteHashAlgorithm": "{{quoteHashAlgorithm}}",
  "quoteExpiresAt": "{{quoteExpiresAt}}",
  "idempotencyKey": "{{e2eVnpayIdempotencyKey}}"
}

exec: pm.test('booking initially waits for online payment', () => pm.expect(['PENDING_PAYMENT','CREATED']).to.include(result.status));
pm.test('8.8 no driver assigned before online payment success', () => pm.expect(pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eVnpayAssignedDriverId')).to.be.oneOf(['', null, undefined]));

description.content: Run 2-5 seconds after 8.8. Expected: Payment Service consumed payment.requested and created a PRE_TRIP VNPAY transaction. If status is PENDING, open the captured payUrl in a browser/sandbox and complete payment before running the driver assignment steps. After valid VNPay return/IPN, Payment Service publishes payment.completed, Booking moves PENDING_PAYMENT -> MATCHING, then publishes ride.created.

exec: pm.test('8.9 pre-trip payment is pending or already success', () => pm.expect(["PENDING","SUCCESS"]).to.include(result.status));
pm.test('8.9 payUrl captured when pending', () => { if (result.status === 'PENDING') pm.expect(pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eVnpayPayUrl')).to.not.be.empty; });

item.name: 8.10 Poll Driver Current Ride After Payment Success

description.content: Run only after VNPay payment is completed and Booking has consumed payment.completed. Expected: payment.completed -> Booking MATCHING -> ride.created -> Matching assignment. Current ride should point at the E2E booking for the same driver token used by this collection. If booking.assignedDriverId differs from driverUserId, Matching selected another online driver from Redis; set other drivers offline/clear stale Redis statuses or use that assigned driver token. Assignment TTL is 30 seconds.

exec: pm.test('8.10 current ride poll returns ok/not-found while async', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 404]));

pm.test('8.10 booking was assigned to this logged-in driver', () => { const assignedDriverId = pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eVnpayAssignedDriverId'); if (assignedDriverId) pm.expect(assignedDriverId).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('driverUserId')); });
pm.test('8.10 driver has assigned ride before timeout', () => pm.expect(rideId, 'rideId from driver current-ride').to.not.be.empty);

description.content: Driver Service accepts the assigned job through the assignment command and publishes ride.accepted. Ride Service consumes ride.accepted and owns the ASSIGNED -> ACCEPTED ride-state transition before arrive/start/complete. Booking also consumes the event. Wait 1-3 seconds before ride lifecycle requests.

exec: pm.test('8.10 booking assigned driver matches current driver', () => { const assignedDriverId = pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eVnpayAssignedDriverId'); if (assignedDriverId) pm.expect(assignedDriverId).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('driverUserId')); });
pm.test('8.10 booking id exists before accept', () => pm.expect(pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eVnpayBookingId')).to.not.be.empty);

description.content: Ride Service validates assigned driver, updates ACCEPTED -> PICKUP, and publishes ride.arrived.
Ride Service validates assigned driver, updates PICKUP -> IN_PROGRESS, and publishes ride.started.
Ride Service validates assigned driver, updates IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED, and publishes ride.completed. Wait 2-5 seconds afterward for Booking and Payment consumers.

exec: pm.test('ride completed status returned when present', () => { if (result.status) pm.expect(result.status).to.eql('COMPLETED'); });

item.name: 8.14 Verify VNPAY Payment Transaction After Ride Completed

description.content: Expected after the prepaid VNPay flow: transaction already exists from payment.requested and should be SUCCESS before/after ride.completed. ride.completed should not charge the customer again; it only lets Payment Service settle driver earning.

exec: pm.test('VNPAY payUrl was captured during pre-trip step when gateway returned one', () => pm.expect(pm.collectionVariables.get('e2eVnpayPayUrl')).to.be.a('string'));

description.content: Expected completed booking appears in customer history with status COMPLETED. VNPay payment success and driver settlement are owned by Payment Service.

exec: const list = Array.isArray(result) ? result : (result.content || result.data || result.items || []);
const booking = list.find(b => (b.id || b.bookingId || b.rideId) === bookingId);
pm.test('8.15 booking appears in history', () => pm.expect(booking, 'booking in history').to.exist);
pm.test('8.15 booking payment method is VNPAY', () => pm.expect(booking.paymentMethod).to.eql('VNPAY'));
pm.test('8.15 booking final status is COMPLETED', () => pm.expect(booking.status).to.eql('COMPLETED'));

description.content: Expected after ride.completed consumer: no active current ride for the driver. If Kafka is still processing, wait and retry.

exec: pm.test('8.16 driver current ride released or still processing', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 404]));
pm.test('8.16 current ride empty when cleanup finished', () => pm.expect(rideId || '').to.be.oneOf(['', null]));

description.content: Main E2E flows for the refactored boundaries: Booking publishes ride.created for matching, and online prepaid bookings publish payment.requested first. Payment Service consumes payment.requested for PRE_TRIP online payment and ride.completed for POST_TRIP cash/settlement. Driver Service only publishes driver.accepted/driver.rejected, Ride Service owns trip lifecycle and publishes ride.arrived/ride.started/ride.completed. Run folders top to bottom. For Kafka async steps, run poll requests after a short wait.
DEPRECATED: Use apiGatewayUrl. Set to gateway port so existing collections work through gateway.
